title: pelargonium × flammeum
intro: Pelargonium × flammeum, also known as the Cape Marigold or Red Hot Poker, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Pelargonium × flammeum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Pelargonium × flammeum is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used externally as a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
